Statement: County approval of DEL-001-01 (Preliminary Architectural Design) required before IFC production of floor plans. Explicit hold point in Procedure.
County approval of preliminary architectural design is required before committing to final exterior elevation geometry.
County approval of preliminary design (DEL-001-01) is a gate condition before Phase B (IFC) of building sections can proceed.
County approval of DEL-001-01 required before IFC issuance of interior elevations.
County approval of preliminary architectural design is required before IFC reflected ceiling plans can be issued.
County approval of preliminary design required before IFC door/window schedule can proceed.
County approval of Preliminary Design is required before Finish Schedule can be issued for construction (IFC gate).
County approval of preliminary architectural design (DEL-001-01) is required before proceeding to IFC demolition plans.
County approval of preliminary architectural design (DEL-001-01) is required before finalizing the specification.
County-approved preliminary architectural design required; structural framing must coordinate with approved floor plan and room layout
Architectural preliminary layout needed to confirm room dimensions and panel space allocation and overhead door locations for circuit planning
Preliminary architectural design must be advanced enough to support permit application with site plan and building information
Confirmed plan dimensions from floor plans must exist before sections are finalized; section cut marks must match floor plan.
Confirmed floor plan room layout and room IDs from DEL-001-02 are a required input before Finish Schedule production can begin.
Architectural floor plan required to establish column grid and bay dimensions for dimensioned plate layout
Required input: confirm stair location and stair clear width and architectural intent
Architectural floor plans required as base drawing for HVAC layout (room dimensions door locations structural grid)
DEL-003-07 requires final architectural floor plans before finalizing equipment placement and spatial coordination
Required for spatial coordination and as drawing underlay for power plan sheets
Architectural floor plans (DEL-001-02) required for drawing background and dimension confirmation
DEL-004-05 requires dimensioned architectural floor plan base from DEL-001-02 before receptacle layout can be produced
Architectural Floor Plans required for coordination of low-voltage wiring routes with wall/partition layouts
DEL-006-04 requires architectural floor plans (DEL-001-02) for spatial coordination of utility room and building layout
Architectural floor plans required before fixture schedule production; room layouts define fixture locations
DEL-006-07 requires preliminary architectural floor plan (room dimensions; occupancy) for occupancy-based fixture counts
DEL-006-08 requires architectural floor plan issued for plumbing coordination -- confirms washroom/locker room/wash station layouts and drain rough-in locations
Architectural IFC drawings issued and coordinated with structural drawings including door/window schedule and renovation plans
IFC architectural floor plans required for bay dimension confirmation
IFC Architectural Drawings approved and P.Eng.-stamped required before construction
IFC door schedule required for overhead door procurement
IFC Door & Window Schedule must be issued before procurement and installation of entrance/exit doors can proceed
Finish schedule (DEL-001-08) must be approved before floor/wall/ceiling finishes can be applied
Renovation plans must be predicated on post-demolition conditions documented in DEL-001-09. DEL-001-09 is the predecessor demolition document.
IFC demolition drawings from Architect required before construction; part of DESIGN-001 prerequisite
Demolition Plans (DEL-001-09) must be issued and approved before demolition phase
Architectural renovation plans required for Old North Shop fixture scope (washroom/urinal/laundry)
IFC renovation drawings from Architect required before construction; part of DESIGN-001 prerequisite
IFC Renovation Plans (DEL-001-10) must be P.Eng.-stamped and issued before construction
IFC Architectural/Renovation Drawings for Old North Shop must include locker room layout; P.Eng.-stamped
IFC Architectural Specification must be issued before procurement and installation; governs materials and installation requirements
County approval of DEL-002-01 (Preliminary Structural Design) required before Foundation Plan IFC issue
County approval of preliminary structural design required before IFC drawing production per R-01 s3.3.2
County approval of DEL-002-01 required before final design commences (R-01 §3.3.2)
County approval of preliminary structural design must be obtained before final IFC crane support drawings are issued
Preliminary Structural Design must receive County project team approval before IFC foundation documents are finalized
Preliminary structural design required for permit application
DEL-006-04 requires structural design (DEL-002) to confirm cistern installation type and structural loads
DEL-006-08 requires structural drawings issued for plumbing coordination -- confirms slab penetrations / cistern support / sump pit construction / roof penetrations for vent system
Cannot construct foundation without Foundation Plan (DEL-002-02) issued for construction and P.Eng.-stamped
Structural IFC drawings must be complete P.Eng.-stamped and issued before construction commences
Mezzanine framing must align with overall building framing grid; building grid required before column layout (F-003 readiness check)
Required input: overall structural grid and slab layout needed to locate stair base
Structural framing plans required to confirm crane clearances and equipment support locations and roof penetration feasibility
Structural drawings required for concrete structure penetration locations and conduit routing
IFC structural framing plans required before construction of repair bay scope
IFC Structural drawings showing ceiling beam/purlin locations and load capacities required for fan layout design (Step 3) and structural approval (Step 3.2 HOLD POINT)
IFC structural sections and details required for door frame and bay construction
Mezzanine framing elevation and depth required from structural engineer before mezzanine section is finalized.
Required input: mezzanine top landing elevation and framing layout needed before stair detailing can establish connection interface and load path
P.Eng.-stamped IFC structural drawings for mezzanine framing must be on site and approved before construction
IFC Structural drawings confirming mezzanine load path and partition interface required before partition construction
Service pit depth and structural details required from structural engineer before pit section is finalized.
Service pit dimensions required for ventilation design

IFC structural drawings (DEL-002-06) must be issued for construction and approved before any pit construction work commences. This is the primary design input governing all dimensions and structural details.
Crane runway beam elevations required from structural engineer before crane clearance sections are finalized.
Structural crane runway must be installed and inspected before crane installation can proceed. Crane runway beams and structural supports are provided by PKG-011 / DEL-002-07.
IFC structural drawings (DEL-002-08) must be issued by structural engineer before installation commences
Steel plate embedment plan (DEL-002-08) must be issued and reviewed before wash bay floor pour proceeds — governs plate size thickness material grade and anchor specifications
P.Eng.-stamped IFC structural drawings for stairs must be on site and approved before construction
Minimum concrete compressive strength (f'c) must be specified by Structural Engineer of Record in DEL-002-10
DEL-002-10 must be issued for design load values and load rating basis
Cannot construct foundation without approved Foundation Design Package (DEL-002-11) issued for construction
DEL-002-12 must be issued and reviewed for material grades and welding/anchorage standards and installer qualifications
Structural specification required for framing tolerances and concrete cure criteria
Approved preliminary design (DEL-003-01) required before IFC layout production; County approval must be received
County approval of DEL-003-01 (Preliminary Mechanical Design) is mandatory before IFC issuance of DEL-003-03
County-approved preliminary mechanical design required before IFC drawings are issued
County-approved Preliminary Mechanical Design (DEL-003-01) is a required input before equipment selections are committed
County approval of Preliminary Mechanical Design (DEL-003-01) required before final calculations
Mechanical preliminary design needed to confirm exhaust fan quantities and locations and motor sizes for circuit allocation
DEL-003-02 (HVAC Layout Plans) must be substantially complete with equipment locations fixed before ductwork routing can be finalized
HVAC Layout Plans (DEL-003-02) must be IFC and P.Eng. stamped before procurement
Approved IFC HVAC Layout Plans required before procurement and installation can proceed
IFC Mechanical Drawings (HVAC Plans) must be approved and P.Eng.-stamped before MUA procurement and installation can begin
HVAC system design and mechanical engineering calculations are PKG-003 design scope; IFC mechanical drawings including fan positions required before installation
Ductwork & Distribution Plans (DEL-003-03) must be IFC and P.Eng. stamped before procurement
Approved IFC Ductwork Plans required before procurement; ductwork sizing routing and layout confirmed
IFC Ductwork & Distribution Plans must be approved and P.Eng.-stamped before MUA ductwork fabrication and installation
Approved mechanical design drawings for exhaust system (Issued for Construction) required before installation can proceed
DEL-003-07 requires DEL-003-05 equipment selections to specify performance requirements and equipment standards
Mechanical Equipment Schedule (DEL-003-05) must be issued to confirm equipment model and specification
Mechanical Equipment Schedule required for air exchanger model selection and performance confirmation
Mechanical Equipment Schedule must be issued confirming MUA unit make model and capacity before procurement
Approved mechanical equipment schedule confirming fan model capacity and static pressure required before procurement
Calculation package required for equipment sizing that governs unit selection and layout
DEL-003-06 (Mechanical Calculation Package) must provide design airflows before duct sizing can proceed
Fan sizing and airflow calculations required before final drawing completion
Mechanical Calculation Package (DEL-003-06) required before performance values can be entered in the equipment schedule
DEL-003-07 requires DEL-003-06 calculation outputs (heating load ventilation volumes air balance) to populate specification performance values
Mechanical Calculation Package (DEL-003-06) must be accepted to confirm heating capacity requirement
Mechanical Calculation Package required to confirm airflow sizing for facility volume
Mechanical Calculation Package must establish required MUA supply airflow rate reconciled with exhaust volumes before equipment selection and duct sizing
Gas load calculations from mechanical design (DEL-003) required to size gas service tie-in
Mechanical Specification (DEL-003-07) must be issued and approved before equipment procurement can commence
Mechanical Specification required for governing standards equipment requirements installation and testing requirements
Mechanical Specification must be issued before installation -- governs installation standards ductwork insulation and equipment requirements
County approval of Preliminary Electrical Design (DEL-004-01) required before SLD is finalized for IFC issue
County-approved Preliminary Electrical Design must be complete before IFC release
Preliminary electrical design (DEL-004-01) must receive County approval before final Lighting Plans are issued for construction
DEL-004-05 requires County-approved preliminary electrical design (DEL-004-01) before IFC development
Preliminary Electrical Design (DEL-004-01) must receive County approval before final low-voltage plans are issued for construction
County preliminary design approval (DEL-004-01) required before final electrical design can proceed to IFC
Panel hierarchy and distribution structure must be established by SLD before panel schedules can be finalized
IFC Electrical Drawings (DEL-004-02) must be issued, P.Eng.-stamped, before installation proceeds
Panel locations and feeder routing required from Power Distribution Plans
Lighting circuit counts required from Lighting Plans to populate panel schedule
IFC electrical drawings for the lighting system must be complete and stamped by PE before installation begins
Receptacle circuit counts required from Receptacle Layout Plans
IFC Receptacle Layout Plans (DEL-004-05) must be issued for construction before rough-in can begin. These are the binding authority for receptacle locations quantities and circuit assignments.
IFC Panel Schedules (DEL-004-06) must be issued for construction before breaker installation and circuit identification. Required for circuit assignments for all receptacle circuits.
IFC Panel Schedules (DEL-004-06) must be issued, P.Eng.-stamped, before installation proceeds
DEL-015-05 requires IFC Low-Voltage System Plans (DEL-004-07) before installation can proceed. DEL-004-07 defines routing quantities cable types and termination locations.
Load calculations from Calculation Package govern panel and breaker ratings
Electrical Calculation Package (DEL-004-08) required for conductor sizing and load calculations
Electrical load calculation from electrical design (DEL-004) required to size electrical service tie-in

IFC Electrical Specification (DEL-004-09) must be available before device procurement and installation. Required to confirm device grade NEMA configurations GFCI/AFCI requirements installation methods and code edition.
DEL-015-05 requires Electrical Specification (DEL-004-09) before installation. DEL-004-09 defines material and workmanship standards for low-voltage work.
Compass orientation must be confirmed via site survey and civil design before elevation drawings can be titled.
County approval of preliminary civil design (DEL-005-01) must be received before IFC grading design proceeds
County approval of preliminary civil design required before IFC drainage plan is released
County approval of Preliminary Civil Design (DEL-005-01) is required before IFC issue of this deliverable
Preliminary civil design must receive County approval before final IFC sections are stamped
Preliminary Civil Design (DEL-005-01) must be approved by County before final specification can proceed
Preliminary civil design (site grading and drainage) required for permit application site plan
Site Grading Plan grading intent must be confirmed before civil sections depicting vertical geometry and cross-fall slopes can be finalized
IFC site grading plan (DEL-005-02) must be issued before construction; defines stripping limits depths and extents.
IFC Civil Grading Plan (DEL-005-02) must be finalized and issued for construction before grading can proceed
Civil drainage system design finalized and IFC drawings issued — site grading plan required for drainage grades and sump location
Drainage Plan drainage structure types must be confirmed before drainage structure sections and connection details can be drafted
Civil drainage plan IFC drawings required for pipe layout invert elevations and sump connection design
IFC civil drawings (DEL-005-04) must be approved and issued for construction before gravel placement proceeds. This is a formal hold point.
IFC civil drawings (P.Eng.-stamped) must be issued before pad construction; includes pad dimensions; concrete mix design; reinforcement; finished grades/slopes; compaction specification
Civil Specification (DEL-005-07) must issue compaction acceptance criteria before compaction testing can commence
Civil Specification (DEL-005-07) must be issued to define aggregate type gradation depth and compaction standard. Required input before construction.
County-approved DEL-006-01 required before IFC drawings
DEL-006-04 requires approved preliminary plumbing design (DEL-006-01) before IFC issue
Preliminary Plumbing Design (DEL-006-01) must be approved by County before final design proceeds
DEL-006-08 requires preliminary plumbing design (DEL-006-01) to be approved by County before final specification can proceed
IFC Plumbing Drawings — Water Distribution Plans required before procurement and installation
IFC plumbing drawings (DEL-006-03) are required before drain placement sizing and routing can be finalized; explicit hold-point declared before underground rough-in
IFC Plumbing Drawings — Drain & Vent Plans required before procurement and installation
Plumbing IFC drawings required for floor drain specifications and connection to mud sump
IFC plumbing drawings for cistern and pump details must be approved and issued before construction begins. P.Eng.-stamped drawings are a prerequisite for installation.
IFC plumbing drawings (DEL-006-04) must be issued P.Eng.-stamped before installation of DEL-014-03 begins
IFC plumbing drawings (DEL-006-05) must be stamped and approved before installation can commence; governs location invert elevations bedding and connection details
Required for fixture count and types for drain/vent sizing; pipe sizing cannot proceed without fixture unit load data
DEL-006-07 requires co-developed fixture schedule (DEL-006-06) for fixture coverage cross-reference and fixture unit counts
Plumbing Fixture Schedule is the authoritative comprehensive record for all fixture data; required before procurement
DEL-007-02 shall not advance to IFC until DEL-007-01 Preliminary Landscape Design has received County approval
County-approved preliminary landscape design (DEL-007-01) is required before planting plans are issued for construction (IFC).
County approval of DEL-007-01 (Preliminary Landscape Design) must be on file before final specification is issued for construction
Approved Landscape Site Plans (DEL-007-02) provide the spatial framework required as primary input for planting zone layout.
Approved Landscape Site Plans (DEL-007-02) provide material callouts and layout dimensions required for specification content
IFC Landscape Plans (DEL-007-02) must be issued before landscaping phase can commence
Approved Planting & Restoration Plans (DEL-007-03) provide species list and installation standards required for planting specification
IFC Planting & Restoration Plans (DEL-007-03) must be issued before landscaping phase can commence
Preliminary architectural design shall be informed by the geotechnical investigation; foundation approach held open pending geotech results (D-009, variable-price).
Geotechnical report required for foundation design coordination; if not yet available, proceed with normal ground condition assumption per RFP ss4.8.2.
Geotechnical report required before foundation/slab assumptions are made in section drawings.
Geotechnical investigation report required for foundation and floor slab design; may affect architectural specification.
Geotechnical report required before final foundation design; foundation type and bearing capacity TBD pending this report
Geotechnical report required before finalizing foundation/structural loads; impacts soil bearing frost depth and slab subgrade design
Geotech report required for foundation and service pit depth determination (R-01 §4.8.2)
Geotechnical report required before structural design can be finalized — provides bearing capacity / groundwater / frost depth / earth pressure parameters
Geotechnical report required for crane column/foundation bearing capacity before foundation-level details are finalized
Geotechnical investigation required before final slab/foundation design which determines embedment depth and anchor length
Required input: geotechnical investigation report needed for foundation and slab bearing conditions; stair base anchors depend on slab design
Geotechnical report is a required input for foundation calculations; blocking for final foundation design
Geotech report required before final foundation pricing can be negotiated and before IFC design is finalized; design may proceed on assumed conditions for proposal phase
Geotechnical investigation report is an essential data prerequisite for foundation-superstructure interface parameters
Geotechnical report must be complete (final issued) before subgrade/compaction grading notes finalized; preliminary data may support Phase 2 only
Geotech report required for soil permeability frost depth and subsurface drainage conditions to inform drainage design
Geotechnical investigation report required to establish subgrade design parameters for pavement section design
Geotechnical report is required input providing subgrade bearing capacity CBR frost depth soil classification and groundwater depth before civil sections can be finalized
Geotechnical investigation report required before driving surface design and slope stability calculations (Hold Point HP-02). Foundation pricing variable until geotech complete.
Geotechnical Investigation Report (DEL-008-01) required for subgrade bearing capacity and frost design values before civil specification can be finalized
Geotechnical report required before finalizing cistern/septic foundation assumptions
Geotechnical report required for foundation/slab design affecting plumbing penetrations
Required for frost depth data critical for determining minimum burial depth of exterior drain lines and mud sump connection
DEL-006-04 requires geotech report (DEL-008-01) if cistern is below-grade; informs material selection
DEL-006-08 requires geotechnical investigation report to inform cistern foundation/support design and septic tank installation conditions
DEL-007-01 requires geotechnical investigation data (soil conditions and bearing capacity) from DEL-008-01 as a design input; may not be available at preliminary stage
DEL-007-02 requires geotechnical report (DEL-008-01) to inform grading and surface design decisions
Geotechnical report provides subsoil characterization for grading pad design and planting soil conditions
Cannot finalize foundation design or construction until complete geotechnical investigation report (PKG-008) is received and reviewed
Geotechnical investigation report must be available and structural design assumptions confirmed or updated accordingly
Geotechnical report required as design input for pad areas; subgrade bearing capacity affects cement pad thickness/reinforcement and gravel pad depth/composition
Geotechnical report required before foundation price negotiation (deferred Step 6)
Preliminary architectural design shall be informed by preliminary site survey for building footprint and site orientation confirmation.
Preliminary site survey required for site orientation, existing building dimensions, and setback confirmation.
Site survey required for siting; affects specification coordination.
Preliminary survey results required as input for existing topography and site control; if not available design proceeds on stated assumptions
Preliminary survey must be complete before grading design can commence; provides existing topography and control points
Preliminary survey required for existing site topography and pre-development drainage baseline
Preliminary survey of site required for existing ground control and topographic base drawing
Preliminary survey data and existing grade elevations are required input for drafting elevation-referenced sections and confirming cut/fill depths
Preliminary site survey required before grading and drainage calculations (Hold Point HP-01). Existing grades and site boundaries needed for drainage basin delineation.
Preliminary Site Survey (DEL-008-02) required for existing grades and topography inputs to civil specification
Preliminary site survey required for site layout and sewer/drain invert elevations
Site survey required for plumbing design
DEL-006-08 requires preliminary site survey to establish existing septic tank location for removal coordination
DEL-007-01 requires preliminary site survey data (topographic context and grading interface) from DEL-008-02 as a design input; may not be available at preliminary stage
DEL-007-02 requires site survey and existing conditions documentation (DEL-008-02) for accurate site representation
DEL-008-02 Preliminary Site Survey must be complete and control point data available before construction survey commences
DEL-008-04 requires DEL-008-03 Construction Survey records including control point coordinates descriptions and datum reference as the basis for as-built survey control
Site survey with benchmark must be established before grading commences
DEL-009-01 (Development Permit) must be approved before building permit application is submitted. Hard gate. Conditions from DEL-009-01 also flow into building permit application content.
Development permit conditions required as input to populate the register. Register cannot be fully populated until DEL-009-01 is obtained.
Development permit must be in place before construction commences
Development permit application and approval required before construction of locker room
Building permit conditions required as input to populate the register. Register cannot be fully populated until DEL-009-02 is obtained.
Building permit must be in place before foundation construction commences
Building permit must be obtained before construction commences
Building permit must be obtained before construction commences
Building permit (DEL-009-02) must be obtained before construction of wash bay enclosure proceeds
Building permit required before construction commences
Safety Code permit requirements required as input to populate the register. Register cannot be fully populated until DEL-009-03 is obtained.
Safety Code permits must be in place before foundation construction commences
All applicable Safety Code permits must be obtained
Safety Code permits must be obtained before construction commences
Alberta Safety Code plumbing permit must be obtained before plumbing rough-in begins
Safety code permits (plumbing/electrical/mechanical) required before respective construction phases
Safety Code permits for gas and electrical work must be obtained before installation commences
Foundation construction must be complete inspected and accepted before superstructure erection. Foundation as-built dimensions and anchor bolt locations verified against IFC drawings.
Foundation construction must be accepted before superstructure and envelope work
Foundation construction (DEL-010-01) must be complete at the wash bay location before wash bay floor and wall work can proceed
Concrete superstructure must be erected and cured before repair bay enclosure work can proceed
Structural openings in concrete superstructure must be complete before door frame installation can commence
Concrete superstructure (DEL-011-01) must be in place before wash bay enclosure installation proceeds
Superstructure must be structurally complete and cured before mezzanine framing loads can be applied
Building shell (PKG-011) must be structurally complete before utility room interior construction can commence
Building structure and utility room walls/slab must be constructed to stage required for cistern placement before cistern installation
Building structure and ceiling must be substantially complete in each zone prior to fixture installation
Steel plate embedments in repair bay floor must be placed or coordinated before floor finishes
Steel embedments for mezzanine attachment points must be installed and verified prior to mezzanine framing
Mezzanine structure (DEL-011-07) above parts room must be structurally complete before partition construction; mezzanine-partition structural interface verification required (HOLD POINT)
Utility Room (DEL-012-02) must be substantially complete before heating system installation can proceed
Utility Room must be structurally complete weathertight and available before equipment installation
Utility Room must be substantially complete (floor walls roof electrical rough-in) before MUA unit can be set in place
MUA supply volume must balance total exhaust volumes from DEL-013-04; exhaust volume data required before MUA can be sized
MUA supply volume must balance total exhaust volumes from DEL-013-05; exhaust volume data required before MUA can be sized
Cistern installation (DEL-014-01) must be at a stage that permits connection of the bulk fill line to the cistern
DEL-014-01 (Cistern & Distribution) supply piping must be roughed-in and pressure tested before emergency shower supply branch can be connected; cistern must be operational before commissioning
Cistern and distribution system must be installed and supply piping roughed-in to fixture zones before fixture final connections
Septic system installed and sanitary drain/DWV rough-in available at industrial wash station location
DEL-014-04 (Floor Drains & Sump Drains) floor drain below emergency shower location must be installed before activating flow test
Lighting circuits shall not be energized until three-phase power service (DEL-015-01) is installed and approved
Three-phase power service (DEL-015-01) must be energized before receptacle circuits can be commissioned. Main distribution panelboard(s) must be installed and branch circuit capacity available.
Three-phase power service (DEL-015-01) must be complete and energized before equipment circuits can be energized
Electrical power supply circuit for heating system must be installed and energized before installation
Dedicated electrical power circuit for cranes must be available at crane disconnect location before crane installation and testing. Circuit provided by PKG-015 / DEL-015-04 (SOW-0059).
Crane manufacturer specifications (wheel loads runway gauge bridge span CMAA service class) are an irrefutable prerequisite for crane runway structural design
IFC Crane drawings showing crane rail elevation and maximum hook path envelope required for fan layout design to establish clearance
DEL-018-01 (Topsoil Stripping) must be complete before grading commences
Topsoil stripping of all driving surface areas must be complete before gravel placement begins
Topsoil stripping (DEL-018-01) must be complete in pad areas before pad construction commences
Site grading and sub-base formation must be confirmed complete before gravel placement
Site grading (DEL-018-02) must be sufficiently complete and subgrade approved before pad construction
Site Grading must be substantially complete to establish drainage grades before wash bay drainage construction can proceed
Site grading must be substantially complete in utility corridor areas before trenching commences
OH&S plan (DEL-019-01) must be initiated to inform safety code audit scope and permit applications
Prime Contractor designation must be established and OH&S program active before construction
DEL-019-01 (Prime Contractor Designation) must be complete before weekly meeting governance can be formally initialized. Governance and authority structures are required inputs.
Prime Contractor designation must be held before any Subcontractors mobilise on site; OH&S program content addressing subcontractors derives from DEL-019-01
DEL-019-01 must be established before QC authority structure can be confirmed; QC program draws authority from OH&S program
QC management verification (DEL-019-04) must be complete before commissioning
DEL-020-01 must be substantially complete before final inspection; written confirmation of commissioning completion required
CCC issuance and final inspection records from DEL-020-02 are required prerequisites; CCC is an explicit gate for lien holdback release package submission
CCC must be issued and signed by Owner before warranty period begins; CCC date is the warranty period start trigger
DEL-021-01 (Bid Security) establishes surety relationship via Consent of Surety; required input before performance bond procurement can proceed
Performance Bond (50%) and Labour & Material Payment Bond (50%) must be in place before construction
Performance and Payment Bonds must be provided with or within 10 days of executed contract
Insurance coverage per RFP must be in place with County named as additional insured
Insurance Certificates with Camrose County named as additional insured are required before contract execution and must be maintained throughout the project.
Design-Builder's own insurance covering independent Subcontractors (min $5M GL and $5M employer liability) must be verified before any Subcontractor onboarding begins
Insurance documentation must be in place and accepted by County before contract execution; DEL-021-04 cannot proceed without completed insurance package
Insurance documentation must be in place as a condition of contract execution
Contract must be executed before construction commences
Contract award and CCDC 14-2013 contract execution is required before Prime Contractor Designation Form can be executed and before work commences.
